summary: A significant limitation of using dry heat for sterilization is its extended time requirement; typically taking around an hour versus only a few minutes with more efficient techniques such as autoclaving via moist heat. This lengthy process can be impractical especially when rapid turnaround times are necessary during medical procedures or production cycles requiring frequent equipment disinfection/sterilization steps. \boxed{} Agent1 concludes by emphasizing how this long processing period poses practical challenges particularly where quick turnarounds might otherwise enable faster workflow efficiencies
